title: How to use the geneticae package
text: Installing package. install released version geneticae CRAN: can install development version GitHub repo : Loading package. geneticae package installed, needs loaded : Help files. Detailed information geneticae package functions can obtained help files using help(package="geneticae"). help file function, example imputation can obtained using ?imputation help(imputation).
code: install.packages("geneticae") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("jangelini/geneticae") library(geneticae)

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: Understanding relationship crops performance environment key problem plant breeders geneticists. advanced stages breeding programs, genotypes evaluated, multi-environment trials (MET) one used experiments. studies test number genotypes multiple environments order identify superior genotypes according performance. experiments, crop performance modeled function genotype (G), environment (E) genotype-environment interaction (GEI). presence GEI generates differential genotypic responses different environments (Angelini et al., 2019; Crossa, 1990; Kang Magari, 1996). Therefore appropriate statistical methods used obtain adequate GEI analysis, essential plant breeders (Giauffret et al., 2000). average performance genotypes different environments can considered absence GEI (Yan Kang, 2003). However, GEI almost always present comparison mean performance genotypes enough. widely used methods analyze MET data based regression models, analysis variance (ANOVA) multivariate techniques. particular, two statistical models widely used among plant breeders provide useful graphical tools study GEI: Additive Main effects Multiplicative Interaction model (AMMI) (Kempton, 1984; Gauch, 1988) Site Regression Model (SREG) (Cornelius et al., 1996; Gauch Zobel, 1997). However, models always efficient enough analyze MET data structure plant breeding programs. present serious limitations presence atypical observations missing values, occur frequently. overcome , several imputation alternatives, robust AMMI (Rodrigues et al., 2016) SREG alternative (Angelini et al., 2022) recently proposed literature. geneticae package created gather one place useful functions type analysis also implements new methodology can found recent literature. importantly, geneticae first package implement robust AMMI models proposed Rodrigues et al. (2016), robusts SREG proposed Angelini et al. (2022) new imputation methods proposed Angelini et al. (2024). addition, need preprocess data use geneticae package, case previous packages require data frame matrix containing genotype environment means genotypes rows environments columns. package, data long format required. restriction columns names genotypes, environments, repetitions () phenotypic traits interest. Also, extra information used analysis may present dataset. Finally, geneticae offers wide variety options customize biplots, part graphical output methods.

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: geneticae package utilizes two datasets illustrate methodology included analyse MET data. yan.winterwheat dataset: yield 18 winter wheat varieties grown nine environments Ontario 1993. Although four blocks replicas environment performed experiment, yield mean variety environment combination available dataset obtained agridat package (Wright, 2020). plrv dataset: resistance study PLRV (Patato Leaf Roll Virus) causing leaf curl. 28 genotypes experimented 6 locations Peru. clone evaluated three times environment, yield, plant weight plot registered. dataset available geneticae obtained agricolae package (de Mendiburu, 2020).
code: library(agridat) data(yan.winterwheat) head(yan.winterwheat) ##   gen  env yield ## 1 Ann BH93 4.460 ## 2 Ari BH93 4.417 ## 3 Aug BH93 4.669 ## 4 Cas BH93 4.732 ## 5 Del BH93 4.390 ## 6 Dia BH93 5.178 data(plrv) head(plrv) ##   Genotype Locality Rep WeightPlant WeightPlot    Yield ## 1   102.18     Ayac   1   0.5100000       5.10 18.88889 ## 2   104.22     Ayac   1   0.3450000       2.76 12.77778 ## 3   121.31     Ayac   1   0.5425000       4.34 20.09259 ## 4   141.28     Ayac   1   0.9888889       8.90 36.62551 ## 5   157.26     Ayac   1   0.6250000       5.00 23.14815 ## 6    163.9     Ayac   1   0.5120000       2.56 18.96296

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: AMMI model (Gauch, 1988) widely used analyse effect GEI. model includes two stages. First, ANOVA performed obtain estimates additive main effects environments genotypes. Secondly, residuals ANOVA arranged matrix genotypes rows environments columns singular value decomposition (SVD) applied order explore patterns related GEI, still present residuals. result first two multiplicative terms SVD often presented biplot called GE represents two-rank approximation GEI effects. rAMMI() function returns GE biplot. Data long format required function, .e. row corresponds one observation column one variable (genotype, environment, repetition () observed phenotype). genotype evaluated environment, phenotypic mean combination genotype environment internally calculated model estimated. Extra variables used analysis may present dataset. Missing values allowed (can imputated, see ). GE biplot yan.winterwheat dataset shown Figure 1 along sentence used obtain . first argument input dataset, names columns necessary information apply technique found also model obtained indicated. Optionally, percentage GEI explained GE biplot can added footnote footnote = T, well tittle titles = T. example, BH93, KE93 OA93 environments contribute interaction vectors longest ones. cultivars m12 Kat present similar interaction patterns (markers close biplot) different Ann Aug, example. closeness cultivar Dia environment BH93 indicates strong positive association , means BH93 extremely favorable environment genotype. OA93 Luc markers opposite, environment considerably unfavorable genotype. Finally, Cas Reb close origin, means adapt equally environments. Figure 1: GE biplot based yield data 1993 Ontario winter wheat performance trials. 71.66% GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. Cultivars shown lowercase environments uppercase. AMMI model, standard form, assumes outliers present data. overcome problem data contamination outlying observations, Rodrigues et al. (2016) proposed five robust AMMI models, can obtained two stages: () fitting robust regression model M-Huber estimator (Huber, 1981) replace ANOVA model; (ii) using robust SVD principal components analysis (PCA) procedure replace standard SVD. now, robust AMMI models available R package. robust biplots proposed Rodrigues et al. (2016) can obtained using rAMMI(). argument type can used specify type model fitted ("rAMMI", "hAMMI", "gAMMI", "lAMMI" "ppAMMI").0 Since sample yan.winterwheat dataset present outliers, conclusions obtained robust biplots differ made classic biplot (Rodrigues et al., 2016). Thus, interpretation robust biplots presented tutorial.
code: rAMMI_clasic <- rAMMIModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype = "gen", environment = "env",        response = "yield", type = "AMMI")  rAMMIPlot(rAMMI_clasic, titles = TRUE, footnote = TRUE)

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: Site Regression model (SREG, also called genotype plus genotype--environment model GGE model) another powerful tool analysis interpretation MET data breeding programs. case, ANOVA performed obtain estimates additive main effects environments SVD performed residuals matrix order explore patterns related genotype (G) GEI. However, ANOVA SVD sensitive atypical observations, common MET. overcome problem, three robust models proposed Angelini et al. (2022) obtain valid results even presence outliers. current package implements methods, allowing users perform robust SREG analysis easily. yan.winterwheat dataset present outliers, conclusions obtained robust biplots differ made classic biplot (Angelini et al., 2022). Thus, interpretation robust biplots presented tutorial. rAMMI() function, GGEmodel() data needs presented long format repetitions extra variables dataset allowed. combinations genotypes environments must present. output GGEmodel() list following elements: model: method fitting SREG model: "SREG","CovSREG","hSREG""ppSREG". coordgenotype: plot coordinates genotypes component. coordenviroment: plot coordinates environments component. eigenvalues: vector eigenvalues component. vartotal: overall variance. varexpl: percentage variance explained component. labelgen: genotype names. labelenv: environment names. axes: axis labels. Data: centered input data. SVP: SVP method. result first two multiplicative terms SVD often presented GGE biplot (Yan et al., 2000), represents rank-two approximation G + GEI effects. Plant breeders found GGE biplots useful tools analysis mega-environment (Yan et al., 2001; Yan Rajcan, 2002) genotype environment evaluation (Bhan et al., 2005; Kang et al., 2006; Yan et al., 2007). GGE biplot addresses many issues relative genotype test environment evaluation. Considering average performance genotype, plot can used evaluate specific general adaptation. addition, environments can visually grouped according ability discriminate among genotypes representativeness test environments. GGE biplot reveals -won-pattern allows recommend specific genotypes environment (Yan Tinker, 2005). Using output GGEmodel(), GGEPlot() builds several GGE biplots views, cultivars shown lowercase environments uppercase. plot also displays methods used centering, scaling SVD. Optionally, percentage G + GEI explained two axes can added footnote footnote = T, well tittle titles = T. basic biplot produced option type="Biplot" (Figure 2). example 78% G + GE variability explained fist two multiplicative terms. angles genotypes markers environments vectors considered understand plot. Thus, example, Kat performs average environments, angle greater 90°° environments. hand, Fun presents -average performance locations except OA93 KE93, indicated acute angles. length environment vectors measure environment’s ability discriminate crops. Figure 2: GGE biplot based yield data 1993 Ontario winter wheat performance trials. scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning (default). 78% G + GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. Cultivars shown lowercase environments uppercase. Breeders usually want identify suitable cultivars particular environment interest, .e., OA93. GGE biplots, Yan Kang (2003) suggest drawing line passes environment marker biplot origin, may referred OA93 axis. performance cultivars particular environment can ranked projecting onto axis. can done setting type = "Selected Environment" providing name environment (OA93) selectedE (Figure 3). Thus, OA93, highest-yielding cultivar Zav, lowest-yielding cultivar Luc. line passes biplot origin perpendicular OA93 axis separates genotypes yielded mean environment. Figure 3: comparison cultivar performance selected environment (OA93). scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning (default). 78% G + GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. Another goal plant breeders determine suitable environment genotype. Yan Kang (2003) suggest plotting line passes origin cultivar marker, .e., Kat. obtain GGE biplots view argument type = "Selected Genotype" selectedG = "Kat" must indicated (Figure 4). Environments classified along genotype axis direction indicated arrow. perpendicular axis separates environments cultivar presented performance average. example, Kat presented performance average environments studied. Figure 4: comparison performance cultivar Luc different environments. scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning (default). 78% G + GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. also possible compare two cultivars, .e. Kat Cas, linking line segment perpendicular . obtain GGE biplots view argument type = "Comparison Genotype" genotypes compared selectedG1 = "Kat" selectedG2 = "Cas" must indicated (Figure 5). Cas yielding Kat environments side perpendicular line Cas. Figure 5: comparison cultivars Kat Cas. scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning (default). 78% G + GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. Cultivars shown lowercase environments uppercase. polygonal view GGE biplots provides effective way visualize -won-pattern MET data (Figure 6). Cultivars vertices polygon (Fun,Zav, Ena, Kat Luc) longest vectors, respective directions, measure ability respond environments. vertex cultivars , therefore, among responsive cultivars; cultivars less responsive respective directions. dotted lines perpendicular polygon sides divide biplot mega-environments, vertex cultivar, one highest yield (phenotype) environments found . OA93 KE93 sector, separated rest biplot two perpendicular lines, Zav highest-yielding cultivar sector. Fun highest-yielding cultivar sector, contains seven environments, namely, EA93, BH93, HW93, ID93, WP93, NN93, RN93. environments fell sectors Ena, Kat, Luc vertex cultivars. indicates vertex cultivars best test environments. Moreover, cultivars poorest environments. Figure 6: polygon view GGE biplot, showing cultivars presented highest yield environment. scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning (default). 78% G + GE variability explained first two multiplicative terms. Cultivars shown lowercase environments uppercase. Selecting cultivars within mega-environments issue among plant breeders. Figure 6 clearly suggests Zav best cultivar OA93 KE93, Fun best cultivar locations. However, breeders select single cultivar megaenvironment. Instead, evaluate cultivars order get idea performance (yield stability). GGE biplot also possible visualize mean yield stability genotypes yield units per se (Figure 7 8). GGE biplot based genotype-focused scaling, obtained indicating SVP = "row" GGEmodel(), provides useful way visualize mean performance stability tested genotypes. unit axes genotypes original unit data. Visualization mean stability genotypes achieved drawing average environment coordinate (AEC). example, Figure 7 shows AEC mega-environment composed environments BH93, EA93, HW93, ID93, NN93, RN93, WP93. abscissa represents G effect, thus, cultivars ranked along AEC abscissa. Cultivar Fun clearly highest-yielding cultivar, average, mega-environment, followed Cas Har,Kat poorest. AEC ordinate approximate GEI associated genotype, measure variability instability genotype. Concentric circles help visualize distances. example, Fun closest one ideal crop, therefore desirable one, followed Cas Hay, turn followed Rum, Ham, Rub, Zav, Del Reb, etc. Figure 8: Classification genotypes respect ideal genotype. code: GGE1 <- rSREGModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype = "gen", environment = "env",                   response = "yield") rSREGPlot(GGE1, type = "Biplot", footnote = F, titles = F) rSREGPlot(GGE1, type = "Selected Environment", selectedE = "OA93",          footnote = F, titles = F) rSREGPlot(GGE1, type = "Selected Genotype", selectedG = "Kat",          footnote = F, titles = F) rSREGPlot(GGE1, type = "Comparison of Genotype",          selectedG1 = "Kat", selectedG2 = "Cas",          footnote = F, titles = F, axis_expand = 1.5) rSREGPlot(GGE1, type = "Which Won Where/What", footnote = F,         titles = F, axis_expand = 1.5) data <- yan.winterwheat[yan.winterwheat$env %in% c("BH93", "EA93","HW93", "ID93",                                                    "NN93", "RN93", "WP93"), ] data <- droplevels(data) GGE2 <- rSREGModel(data, genotype = "gen", environment = "env",                   response = "yield", SVP = "row")  rSREGPlot(GGE2, type = "Mean vs. Stability", footnote = F, titles = F, sizeEnv = 0) rSREGPlot(GGE2, type = "Ranking Genotypes", footnote = F, titles = F, sizeEnv = 0) GGE3 <- rSREGModel(data, genotype = "gen", environment = "env",                   response = "yield", SVP = "column") rSREGPlot(GGE3, type = "Relationship Among Environments", footnote = F, titles = F) rSREGPlot(GGE3, type = "Ranking Environments", footnote = F, titles = F)

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: previous example used dataset without contamination, MET data often contain atypical observations due environmental stress, pests, measurement errors. illustrate robust capabilities package, refer study Angelini et al. (2022) using lavoranti.eucalyptus dataset, available agridat package (Wright 2020). case, distance-distance plot identified five atypical genotypes (1, 2, 21, 22, 24). noted Angelini et al. (2022), outliers present, first PC eigenvalues classic SREG model often overestimated. occurs standard SREG uses measures variability highly influenced extreme values; consequently, outliers direction first principal axes inflate corresponding variances artificially increase proportion explained variability. context, high percentage variability explained classic biplot can spurious, reflecting presence outliers rather actual main structure data. package allows fitting robust alternatives overcome inflation simply changing method argument. following code compares classic SREG robust approaches: Figure 11: GGE biplot based height 25 progenies Eucalyptus grandis trials carried seven environments southern southeastern regions Brazil. scaling method used symmetrical singular value partitioning. SREG (), hSREG (B), CovSREG (C), ppSREG (D). display genotypes environments shows different behavior classic SREG biplot. standard SREG biplot shows overlap genotype markers direction first PC due dominant effect observations 2, 21, 22 24, difficult interpretation analysis MET data (Figure 11 ). example, length environ­ mental vectors measure environment’s ability discriminate cultivars, can conclude environment 2 one discriminating classical biplot analyzed. contrary, near biplot origin robust ones interpreted, indicating lack discriminating ability; , provide information cultivars, therefore, test environment useless (Figure 11 B–D). contradiction occurs influence genotypes 2, 21, 22 24 poorest performance environment (genotypes rendering 22.60 environment give 17.79, 19.77, 20.45 20.36, respectively). Outliers concentrated single environment may result particular climatic event pests/diseases influenced expected performance. considering robust alternatives, impact outlying observations reduced leading easier interpretation, genotype markers dispersed. Therefore, use robust alternatives allowed inclusion genotypes analysis, reducing influence, consequently distorting results.
code: library(patchwork) library(ggplot2) data("lavoranti.eucalyptus")  # Bellthorpe lavoranti_Bellthorpe <- droplevels(subset(lavoranti.eucalyptus, origin=="Bellthorpe"))  Nenv <-length(levels(lavoranti_Bellthorpe$loc)) Ngen <- length(levels(lavoranti_Bellthorpe$gen))  lavoranti_Bellthorpe$genot <- rep(1:Ngen, Nenv) lavoranti_Bellthorpe$genot <- as.factor(lavoranti_Bellthorpe$genot)   # SREG Classic SREG_classic <- rSREGModel(lavoranti_Bellthorpe, genotype = "genot",                            environment = "loc", response = "height",                            model = "SREG")  # hSREG hSREG <- rSREGModel(lavoranti_Bellthorpe, genotype = "genot",                      environment = "loc", response = "height",                      model = "hSREG")  # CovSREG CovSREG <- rSREGModel(lavoranti_Bellthorpe, genotype = "genot",                      environment = "loc", response = "height",                      model = "hSREG")    # ppSREG ppSREG <- rSREGModel(lavoranti_Bellthorpe, genotype = "genot",                      environment = "loc", response = "height",                      model = "ppSREG")  # Graphs p1 <- rSREGPlot(SREG_classic, footnote = F, titles = T) p2 <- hSREG_plot  <- rSREGPlot(hSREG, footnote = F, titles = T) p3 <- CovSREG_plot <- rSREGPlot(CovSREG, footnote = F, titles = T) p4 <- ppSREG_plot  <- rSREGPlot(ppSREG, footnote = F, titles = T)  grafico_robusto_SREG <- (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4) +    plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') &    theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))  grafico_robusto_SREG

title: How to use the geneticae package
text: One major limitation AMMI SREG models require complete two-way data table. Although METs designed genotypes evaluated environments, missing values common due measurement errors destruction plants animals, floods harvest problems. addition, genotypes might incorporated discarded study promising poor performance. imputation() function includes several methods overcome problem missing data, recently published available R package now, recently proposed EM-SREG EM-bSREG methods (Angelini et al., 2024). present example, observations complete yan.winterwheat deleted: impute missing values using methods proposed Angelini et al. (2024) "EM-SREG" "EM-bSREG", use following code: methods available geneticae : "EM-SREG", "EM-bSREG", "EM-SVD", "Gabriel", "WGabriel" "EM-PCA".

title: How to use the geneticae Shiny App

text: Web: https://jangelini.github.io/geneticae/ CRAN: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=geneticae/index.html  Understanding relationship crops performance environment key problem plant breeders geneticists. advanced stages breeding programs, genotypes evaluated, multi-environmental trials (MET) one used experiments. studies test number genotypes multiple environments order identify superior genotypes according performance. experimentes, crop performance modeled function genotype (G), environment (E) genotype-environment interaction (GEI). presence GEI generates differential genotypic responses different environments. Therefore appropriate statistical methods used obtain adequate GEI analysis, essential plant breeders. average performance genotypes different environments can considered absence GEI. However, GEI almost always present comparison mean performance genotypes enough. widely used methods analyze MET data based regression models, analysis variance (ANOVA) multivariate techniques. particular, two statistical models widely used among plant breeders provide useful graphical tools study GEI: Additive Main effects Multiplicative Interaction model (AMMI) Site Regression Model (SREG). However, models always efficient enough analyze MET data structure plant breeding programs. present serious limitations presence atypical observations missing values, occur frequently. overcome , several imputation alternatives (Angelini et al., 2024) robust AMMI SREG model recently proposed literature (Rodrigues et al., 2016; Angelini et al., 2022). geneticae package created gather one place useful functions type analysis also implements new methodology can found recent literature. importantly, geneticae first package implement robust AMMI models proposed Rodrigues et al. (2016), robusts SREG proposed Angelini et al. (2022) new imputation methods proposed Angelini et al. (2024). addition, need preprocess data use geneticae package, case previous packages require data frame matrix containing genotype environment means genotypes rows environments columns. package, data long format required. restriction columns names genotypes, environments, repetitions () phenotypic traits interest. Also, extra information used analysis may present dataset. title: EM-AMMI imputation method — EM.AMMI
text: X data frame matrix genotypes rows environments columns replications experiment. PC.nb number principal components AMMI model used; default value 1. PC.nb=0 main effects used estimate cells data table (interaction ignored). number principal components must greater min(number rows X table, number columns X table)–2. initial.values (optional) initial values missing cells. can single value, used empty cells, vector length equal number missing cells (starting missing values first column). omitted, initial values obtained main effects corresponding model, , grand mean observed data increased (decreased) row column main effects. precision (optional) algorithm converges maximal change values missing cells two subsequent steps greater value (default 0.01). max.iter (optional) maximum permissible number iterations (, number repeats algorithm’s steps 2 5); default value 1000. change.factor (optional) introduced analogy step size gradient descent method, parameter can shorten time executing algorithm decreasing number iterations. change.factor=1 (default) defines previous approximation changed new values missing cells (standard EM-AMMI algorithm). However, change.factor<1, new approximations computed values missing cells changed direction new approximation change smaller. useful changes cyclic thus convergence reached. Usually, argument affect final outcome (, imputed values) compared default value change.factor=1. simplified.model AMMI model contains general mean, effects rows, columns interaction terms. EM-AMMI algorithm step 2 calculates current effects rows columns; effects change iteration iteration empty (outset) cells iteration filled different values. step 3 EM-AMMI uses effects re-estimate cells marked missed (default, simplified.model=FALSE). , however, possible procedure converge. Thus user offered simplified EM-AMMI procedure calculates general mean effects rows columns first iteration next iterations uses values (simplified.model=TRUE). simplified procedure initial values affect outcome (whilst EM-AMMI results usually depend initial values). simplified procedure number iterations convergence usually smaller , furthermore, convergence reached even cases regular procedure fails. regular procedure converge standard initial values (see description argument initial.values), simplified model can used determine better set initial values.

title: EM-AMMI imputation method — EM.AMMI
text: list containing: X: imputed matrix (filled missing values estimated    EM-AMMI procedure); PC.SS: sum squares representing variation explained    principal components (squares eigenvalues singular value    decomposition); iteration: final number iterations; precision.final: maximum change estimated values    missing cells last step iteration (precision convergence)    . algorithm converged, value slightly smaller    argument precision; PC.nb.final: number principal components eventually    used EM.AMMI() function. function checks many    missing cells unambiguously compute parameters SVD    decomposition (Gauch Zobel, 1990). case final number    principal components used smaller passed    function argument PC.nb; convergence: value TRUE means algorithm converged    last iteration.

title: Imputation of missing cells in two-way data sets — imputation
text: Data dataframe containing genotypes, environments, repetitions () phenotypic trait interest. variables used analysis can present. genotype column name containing genotypes. environment column name containing environments. response column name containing phenotypic trait. rep column name containing replications. argument NULL, replications available data. Defaults NULL. type imputation method. Either "EM-AMMI", "EM-GGE", "EM-SREG", "EM-bSREG", "Gabriel", "Eigenvector", "WGabriel", "EM-PCA". Defaults "EM-AMMI". nPC number components used predict missing values. Default 2. initial.values initial values missing cells. can single value vector length equal number missing cells. precision threshold assessing convergence. maxiter maximum number iteration algorithm. change.factor `change.factor` equal 1, previous approximation changed new values (standard EM). Smaller values can help convergence changes cyclic. simplified.model logical. TRUE, calculates effects first iteration speed convergence help cases regular procedure fails. scale boolean. default TRUE "EM-PCA". method "Regularized" "EM" "EM-PCA". row.w row weights "EM-PCA". coeff.ridge ridge coefficient "EM-PCA". seed integer random initialization "EM-PCA". nb.init number random initializations "EM-PCA". Winf lower weight WGabriel. Wsup upper weight WGabriel.

title: Imputation of missing cells in two-way data sets — imputation
text: Often, multi-environment experiments unbalanced several genotypes tested environments. Several methodologies proposed order solve lack balance caused missing values, included function: EM-AMMI: iterative scheme built round procedure used obtain AMMI imputations EM algorithm. additive parameters initially set computing grand mean, genotype means environment means obtained observed data. residuals observed cells initialized cell mean minus genotype mean minus environment mean plus grand mean, interactions missing positions initially set zero. initial multiplicative parameters obtained SVD matrix residuals, missing values filled appropriate AMMI estimates. subsequent iterations, usual AMMI procedure applied completed matrix missing values updated corresponding AMMI estimates. arguments used method :initial.values, precision, maxiter, change.factor simplified.model EM-GGE: Iterative SVD-based imputation focusing G+GE. EM-SREG: Iterative algorithm using Sites Regression model. Supports variants  like standard SVD Bayesian PCA (EM-bSREG). Gabriel: combines regression lower-rank approximation using SVD. method initially replaces missing cells arbitrary values, subsequently imputations refined iterative scheme defines partition matrix missing value turn uses linear regression columns (rows) obtain new imputation. arguments used method dataframe. WGabriel: modification Gabriel method uses weights chosen cross-validation. arguments used method Winf Wsup. EM-PCA: impute missing entries mixed data using iterative PCA algorithm. algorithm first consists imputing missing values initial values. second step iterative PCA algorithm perform PCA completed dataset estimate parameters. , imputes missing values reconstruction formulae order nPC (fitted matrix computed nPC components scores loadings). steps estimation parameters via PCA imputation missing values using fitted matrix iterate convergence. arguments used methods : nPC, scale, method, row.w, coeff.ridge, precision, seed, nb.init maxiter

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
text: Fits classical robust Additive Main effects Multiplicative Interaction (AMMI) model genotype--environment data.

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
code: rAMMIModel(   Data,   genotype = "gen",   environment = "env",   response = "Y",   rep = NULL,   Ncomp = 2,   type = "AMMI" )

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
text: Data dataframe genotypes, environments, repetitions () phenotypic trait interest. variables used analysis can included. genotype column name containing genotypes. Defaults `"gen"`. environment column name containing environments. Defaults `"env"`. response column name containing phenotypic trait interest. Defaults `"Y"`. rep column name containing replications. argument `NULL` (default), assumed data already contains means per genotype environment. provided, means calculated automatically. Ncomp number principal components retain interaction part. Defaults 2. type method fitting AMMI model: `"AMMI"` (classical), `"rAMMI"`, `"hAMMI"`, `"gAMMI"`, `"lAMMI"` `"ppAMMI"` (robust variants). Defaults `"AMMI"`.

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
text: list class rAMMI containing: gen_scores Matrix genotype scores (U * D). env_scores Matrix environment loadings (V). eigenvalues Vector singular values retained components. gen_labels Names genotypes. env_labels Names environments. Ncomp Number principal components used. type fitting method used. vartotal Total variance explained multiplicative terms.

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
text: overcome problem data contamination outlying observations, Rodrigues, Monteiro Lourenco (2015) propose robust AMMI model based M-Huber estimator robust SVD/PCA procedures. `type` argument allows choosing several robust strategies: AMMI: Classical AMMI model using Least Squares standard SVD. rAMMI: Uses L1 norm instead L2 norm compute     robust approximation SVD (via pcaMethods). hAMMI: Uses Hubert's approach (PcaHubert) combining     projection-pursuit robust covariance estimation. gAMMI: Uses Grid search algorithm PCA (PcaGrid). lAMMI: Performs PCA data projected onto unit sphere (PcaLocantore). ppAMMI: Uses projection-pursuit (PcaProj) calculate robust     eigenvalues eigenvectors.

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
text: Rodrigues P.C., Monteiro ., Lourenco V.M. (2015). robust   AMMI model analysis genotype--environment data. Bioinformatics   32, 58–66.

title: Robust AMMI Model Fitting — rAMMIModel
code: library(agridat) data(yan.winterwheat)  # Classical AMMI mod_ammi <- rAMMIModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype = "gen",                         environment = "env", response = "yield", type = "AMMI")  # Robust AMMI (using Hubert's method) mod_rammi <- rAMMIModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype = "gen",                          environment = "env", response = "yield", type = "hAMMI")

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
text: Generates high-quality biplot (PC1 vs PC2) classical   robust AMMI models using ggplot2 framework.

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
code: rAMMIPlot(   model_res,   colGen = "gray47",   colEnv = "darkred",   sizeGen = 6,   sizeEnv = 6,   titles = TRUE,   footnote = TRUE,   axis_expand = 1.2,   limits = TRUE,   axes = TRUE,   axislabels = TRUE )

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
text: model_res object class rAMMI generated rAMMIModel. colGen color genotype labels. Defaults `"gray47"`. colEnv color environment labels vectors. Defaults `"darkred"`. sizeGen text size genotype labels. Defaults 6. sizeEnv text size environment labels. Defaults 6. titles logical. `TRUE` (default), title indicating model type added plot. footnote logical. `TRUE` (default), caption percentage explained GxE variation added bottom. axis_expand numeric value expand axis limits. Useful prevent labels cut . Defaults 1.2. limits logical. `TRUE` (default), uses coord_fixed() maintain 1:1 aspect ratio axes. axes logical. `TRUE` (default), draws dashed horizontal vertical lines passing origin (0,0). axislabels logical. `TRUE` (default), includes axis titles percentage variance explained principal component.

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
text: ggplot2 object. allows customization using   standard ggplot layers (e.g., + theme_bw()).

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
text: biplot constructed using scaling factor 0.5 (symmetric scaling), allows representing genotypes environments algebraic space. Genotypes displayed points (text), environments represented vectors origin.

title: AMMI Biplots with ggplot2 — rAMMIPlot
code: library(agridat) data(yan.winterwheat)  # Classical AMMI mod_ammi <- rAMMIModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype = "gen",                         environment = "env", response = "yield", type = "AMMI")  rAMMIPlot(mod_ammi,sizeGen=4,sizeEnv=4)   data(plrv) mod_ammi_rep <- rAMMIModel(plrv, genotype="Genotype", environment="Locality",                          response="Yield", rep="Rep", type="AMMI") rAMMIPlot(mod_ammi_rep,sizeGen=4,sizeEnv=4)

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
text: Site Regression model (also called genotype +  genotype--environment (GGE) model) powerful tool effective  analysis interpretation data multi-environment trials  breeding programs. different functions R fit SREG model,  however, function following improvements: Includes recently published robust versions SREG model (Angelini et  al., 2022). can used data trials repetitions  (need calculate means beforehand). variables  used analysis can present dataset.

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
code: rSREGModel(   Data,   genotype = "gen",   environment = "env",   response = "yield",   rep = NULL,   model = "SREG",   SVP = "symmetrical" )

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
text: Data dataframe genotypes, environments, repetitions () phenotypic trait interest. Additional variables used model may present data. genotype column name genotypes. environment column name environments. response column name phenotypic trait. rep column name replications. argument NULL, replications data. Defaults NULL. model method fitting SREG model: `"SREG"`,`"CovSREG"`,`"hSREG"` `"ppSREG"` (see References). Defaults `"SREG"`. SVP method singular value partitioning. Either `"row"`, `"column"`, `"symmetrical"`. Defaults `"symmetrical"`.

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
text: list class GGE_Model containing: model SREG model  version. coordgenotype plotting coordinates genotype  every component. coordenviroment plotting coordinates  environment every component. eigenvalues vector eigenvalues  component. vartotal overall variance. varexpl percentage variance explained component. labelgen genotype names. labelenv environment names. axes axis labels. Data scaled centered input data. SVP name SVP method. biplot class ggplot

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
text: linear model robust regression using M estimator proposed  Huber (1964, 1973) fitted iterated re-weighted least squares,  combination three robust SVD/PCA procedures, resulted total  three robust SREG alternatives. robust SVD/PCA considered : CovSREG: robust PCA obtained replacing  classical estimates location covariance robust analogues  using Minimum Regularized Covariance Determinant (MRCD) approach; hSREG: robust PCA method tries combine advantages  approaches, PCA based robust covariance matrix based projection  pursuit; ppSREG: robust PCA uses projection pursuit  directly calculates robust estimates eigenvalues eigenvectors  without going robust covariance estimation. attractive  method bigdata situations, common METs ( genotypes tested large number environments), principal  components can calculated sequentially.

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
text: Julia Angelini, Gabriela Faviere, Eugenia Bortolotto, Gerardo  Domingo Lucio Cervigni & Marta Beatriz Quaglino (2022) Handling outliers  multi-environment trial data analysis: direction robust SREG  model, Journal Crop Improvement, https://doi.org/10.1080/15427528.2022.2051217

title: Site Regression model — rSREGModel
code: library(geneticae)   # Data without replication  library(agridat)  data(yan.winterwheat)  GGE1 <- rSREGModel(yan.winterwheat, genotype="gen", environment="env", response="yield")   # Data with replication  data(plrv)  GGE2 <- rSREGModel(plrv, genotype = "Genotype", environment = "Locality",                   response = "Yield", rep = "Rep")

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
text: GGE biplots used visual examination relationships  test environments, genotypes, genotype--environment  interactions. `rSREGPlot()` produces biplot object class 'ggplot',  using output rSREGModel function.  Several types biplots offered focus different aspects  analysis. Customization options also included. function  modification `rSREGPlot` function  GGEBiplots package.

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
code: rSREGPlot(   rSREGModel,   type = "Biplot",   d1 = 1,   d2 = 2,   selectedE = NA,   selectedG = NA,   selectedG1 = NA,   selectedG2 = NA,   colGen = "gray47",   colEnv = "darkred",   colSegment = "gray30",   colHull = "gray30",   sizeGen = 6,   sizeEnv = 6,   largeSize = 4.5,   axis_expand = 1.2,   axislabels = TRUE,   axes = TRUE,   limits = TRUE,   titles = TRUE,   footnote = TRUE )

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
text: rSREGModel object class rSREGModel. type type biplot produce. "Biplot": Basic biplot. "Selected Environmen"t: Ranking cultivars based  performance given environment. "Selected Genotype": Ranking environments based  performance given cultivar. "Relationship Among Environments". "Comparison Genotype". "Won /": Identifying 'best'  cultivar environment. "Discrimination vs. representativeness": Evaluating  environments based discriminating ability representativeness. "Ranking Environments": Ranking environments respect  ideal environment. "Mean vs. stability": Evaluating cultivars based average  yield stability. "Ranking Genotypes": Ranking genotypes respect  ideal genotype. d1 PCA component plot x axis. Defaults 1. d2 PCA component plot y axis. Defaults 2. selectedE name environment evaluate `type="Selected Environment"`. selectedG name genotype evaluate `type="Selected Genotype"`. selectedG1, name genotype compare `selectedG2`  `type="Comparison Genotype"`. selectedG2, name genotype compare `selectedG1`  `type="Comparison Genotype"`. colGen genotype attributes colour. Defaults `"gray47"`. colEnv environment attributes colour. Defaults `"darkred"`. colSegment segment circle lines colour. Defaults `"gray30"`. colHull hull colour `type="Won /"`. Defaults "gray30". sizeGen genotype labels text size. Defaults 4. sizeEnv environment labels text size. Defaults 4. largeSize larger labels text size use two selected genotypes `type="Comparison Genotype"`, outermost genotypes `type="Won /"`. Defaults 4.5. axis_expand multiplication factor expand axis limits enable fitting labels. Defaults 1.2. axislabels logical, argument `TRUE` labels axes included. Defaults `TRUE`. axes logical, argument `TRUE` x y axes going origin drawn. Defaults `TRUE`. limits logical, argument `TRUE` axes re-scaled. Defaults `TRUE`. titles logical, argument `TRUE` plot title included. Defaults `TRUE`. footnote logical, argument `TRUE` footnote included. Defaults `TRUE`.

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
text: biplot class ggplot

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
text: Yan W, Kang M (2003). GGE Biplot Analysis: Graphical Tool  Breeders, Geneticists, Agronomists. CRC Press. Sam Dumble (2017). GGEBiplots: GGE Biplots 'ggplot2'. R  package version 0.1.1. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=GGEBiplots

title: GGE biplots with ggplot2 — rSREGPlot
code: library(geneticae)   # Data without replication  library(agridat)  data(yan.winterwheat)  GGE1 <- rSREGModel(yan.winterwheat)  rSREGPlot(GGE1,sizeGen=4,sizeEnv=4)    # Data with replication  data(plrv)  GGE2 <- rSREGModel(plrv, genotype = "Genotype", environment = "Locality",                   response = "Yield", rep = "Rep")  rSREGPlot(GGE2,sizeGen=4,sizeEnv=4)

title: geneticae 1.0.1
text: Major improvements rAMMIModel() rAMMIPlot() documentation clarify robust estimation methods return objects. Updated package Description latest references robust SREG (2022) MET data imputation (2024).

title: geneticae 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-16 New features: Included new imputation methods specifically developed multi-environment trials based Angelini et al. (2024). Renamed functions: Several functions renamed descriptive consistent: rAMMI now rAMMIModel, rSREG now rSREGModel, GGE now GGEModel. Bug fixes: Fixed issue rAMMIModel.R datasets replicates handled correctly calculating genotype--environment means. Documentation: Added comprehensive tutorial vignette (“Tutorial”) covering new features SREG robust model.

title: geneticae 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-20 Added SREG robust model GGEmodel() function. Dependecy archived bcv package removed.

title: geneticae 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-09 Dependecy archived GGEBiplotGUI package removed.

title: geneticae 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-12-20 Changed default option “symmetrical” SVP argument GGEmodel() function. Changed agridat package Imports Suggests DESCRIPTION file.

title: geneticae 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-09-16 First submission CRAN.

title: geneticae 0.0.9000
text: first development version package.
